props.heading: Avocado Mastery Awaits
props.subheading: Elevate Your Plate. Transform Your Health. Lead the Movement.
props.imageAlt: AI-generated Hero image featuring fresh avocados cut half

tabs.label: Overview
tabs.content:
From the creamy Hass to the buttery Fuerte, each avocado variety offers unique flavors and textures.

• **Hass Avocados** – The most popular variety worldwide; creamy texture and rich flavor
• **Fuerte Avocados** – Pear-shaped with smooth, mild taste
• **Bacon Avocados** – Thinner skin and milder flavor
• **Reed Avocados** – Large, round, and creamy

Available year-round.

tabs.label: Recipes & Ideas
tabs.content:
• **Breakfast** – Toast, smoothie bowls, eggs, omelets
• **Lunch & Salads** – Grain bowls, salads, seafood
• **Dinner** – Tacos, pasta, stuffed vegetables
• **Snacks & Desserts** – Guacamole, chocolate mousse, vegan ice cream, parfaits

tabs.label: Sustainability
tabs.content:
Progressive growers protect water, soil, and communities through regenerative farming.

• **Water Conservation** – Modern irrigation reduces water use by up to 50%
• **Soil Health** – Regenerative practices build organic matter and support wildlife
• **Reduced Pesticides** – Integrated pest management protects ecosystems and farmworkers
• **Community Impact** – Fair-trade certification ensures fair wages and local investment

Choosing sustainably sourced avocados directly supports farmers and future generations.

tabs.label: Fun Facts
tabs.content:
• **Fruit, Not Vegetable** – Botanically classified as berries with a single large seed
• **Unique Fat Profile** – The only fruit with significant monounsaturated fat
• **Longevity** – Trees live over 100 years and continue producing throughout
• **Slow Growth** – Takes 3–4 years for the first fruit; then decades of abundance
• **Ancient Origins** – Cultivated for 5,000+ years, originating in south-central Mexico

Avocados are nature's golden treasure.

tabs.label: Selection & Storage Tips
tabs.content:
• **Selection** – Gently squeeze for firmness; choose avocados that yield slightly to palm pressure
• **Ripeness** – Use a paper bag with an apple to speed ripening; test with a small knife near the pit
• **Storage** – Keep unripe at room temperature; refrigerate when ripe (lasts 3–5 days)
• **Cut Avocados** – Preserve with lemon juice and plastic wrap; use within 1–2 days

Master these techniques for perfect avocados whenever you need them.

props.title: Discover the Magic of Avocados
props.body:
## Nutritional Benefits of Avocados

Avocados are not only delicious, but also packed with essential nutrients. They're an excellent source of vitamins C, E, K, and B-6, along with riboflavin, niacin, folate, pantothenic acid, magnesium, and potassium.

## Heart-Healthy Fats

Avocados also contain lutein, beta-carotene, and omega-3 fatty acids. Plus, avocados are known for their heart-healthy monounsaturated fats, which may help lower bad cholesterol and reduce the risk of heart disease.

## Enjoy Avocados Today

Experience the magic of avocados in your meals today!

items.a: Absolutely! Avocados are packed with heart-healthy monounsaturated fats, potassium, vitamins C, E, and K, plus fiber. They're a nutrient-dense food that supports heart health and keeps you feeling full. Like any food, balance is key—a quarter to half an avocado per serving is a great way to enjoy the benefits.

items.q: How do I choose a ripe avocado?
items.a: Gently press the avocado in your palm. A ripe avocado should yield slightly to pressure without feeling mushy. If it feels firm, let it sit on your kitchen counter for one to two days to ripen naturally.

Sustainable avocado farming is possible and important. Look for growers who prioritize water conservation and ethical practices. By choosing responsibly grown avocados, you support both your health and the planet.

props.heading: Fresh & Vibrant Oranges
props.subheading: Discover the vibrant world of citrus fruits.

props.title: The Wonders of Oranges
props.body:
**Oranges** are among the most beloved fruits globally, cherished not only for their **delicious taste** but also for their nutritional benefits. Here are some intriguing insights about oranges:

- **Rich in Vitamin C**, oranges play a crucial role in boosting the immune system and maintaining skin health.
- The orange tree, an evergreen species, can reach heights of up to 10 meters and blooms with fragrant flowers.
- With over **600 varieties**, oranges offer a diverse range of flavors and uses.
- Originating in Southeast Asia, oranges have a rich history of cultivation spanning thousands of years.
- Beyond being consumed fresh, oranges are versatile ingredients in cooking, baking, and are essential in producing juices and marmalades.

Discover more about the diverse benefits and culinary uses of oranges on our page.

cards.title: Orange Varieties
cards.description: From bright Valencia to zesty Blood oranges, explore the diverse world of orange cultivars. Each variety brings its own flavor profile and best uses, whether fresh, juiced, or cooked.

items.q: How do I pick a ripe grapefruit?
items.a: Choose grapefruits that feel heavy for their size and have a smooth, unblemished skin. They should yield slightly to gentle pressure, indicating they're juicy and ready to enjoy.

props.heading: Winter Jam Competition
props.subheading: Celebrate homemade flavors. Enter now and compete for glory and prizes.

props.title: Competition Details
props.body:
## How It Works

Our Winter Jam Competition invites home jam makers of all skill levels to showcase their finest creations. Whether you've been preserving for years or just discovered your passion for jam making, this is your moment to shine.

## Eligibility

Open to all amateur jam makers aged 18 and over. Commercial jam producers may enter the professional category. All entries must be homemade and original recipes.

## Judging Criteria

Entries are evaluated on taste (40%), texture and consistency (30%), creativity and innovation (20%), and presentation (10%). Winners in each category are announced in January.

## Submission Details

- **Entry Deadline:** December 20, 2024
- **Submission:** Mail or drop off two 8oz jars of your entry with the completed entry form
- **Entry Fee:** $15 per entry (supports venue and prizes)
- **Categories:** Fruit Preserves, Savory & Spiced, Experimental & Fusion, and Student (under 25)

items.a: The entry fee is $15 per jam entry. This supports the competition venue, judging panel honorariums, and prize awards. Proceeds from entry fees also fund next year's competition expansion.

A panel of 12 expert judges—including professional chefs, food critics, and jam artisans—tastes and evaluates each entry blind (without knowing the maker). Judging is based on taste (40%), texture (30%), creativity (20%), and presentation (10%).

items.q: Can I ship my entry or must I deliver it in person?
items.a: Local entries (within 50 miles) can be dropped off at our collection site or mailed to arrive by December 20. Out-of-area entries must be shipped via insulated mail to ensure freshness. Include a copy of your entry form with your shipment.

The competition is open to all amateur jam makers aged 18+. Professional jam producers may enter the Professional category. All recipes must be original and homemade—store-bought or commercially produced jams are not eligible.
Winners in each category will be announced on January 10, 2025, at our Winter Jam Awards Celebration event. All participants are invited to attend and celebrate.

items.q: Can I use recipes from cookbooks or online sources?
items.a: Yes—you may adapt published recipes, but we encourage innovation. Document your recipe and any modifications you made. Judges appreciate both traditional mastery and creative experimentation.

props.title: Ready to Compete?
props.description: Join jam makers from across the region. Enter by December 20, 2024, and be part of the celebration.
